qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Flask application has no MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH limit, enabling unauthenticated denial of service via large request bodies to the import endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns missing Flask request body size limits and unbounded import processing, not the advisory's backup restore Zip Slip caused by zipfile.extractall() without entry path validation.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Static content handler bypasses SALTED_PASS authentication, exposing watch screenshots and favicons without authorization
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns a static_content authentication check that ignores SALTED_PASS, not the advisory's backup restore ZIP extraction path traversal root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is a Zip Slip arbitrary file overwrite in backup restore via zipfile.extractall(self.datastore_path) without path validation, while the qualifying findings cover request body DoS and SALTED_PASS static content authorization bypass.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-25g8-2mcf-fcx9 describes backup restore Zip Slip through zipfile.extractall() preserving ../ paths in uploaded ZIP archives.
missing_max_content_length/vulnerability_report.json describes missing Flask MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH and unbounded /api/v1/import request processing.
static_content_auth_bypass/vulnerability_report.json describes static_content endpoints bypassing SALTED_PASS authentication checks.
